entries.title: Hippolytus Exegetical Fragments - Scholia on Daniel on daniel 2:11
entries.excerpt: "And they say, There is not a man." Whereas, therefore, they declared it to be impossible that what was asked by the king should be told by man; God showed them, that what is impossible with man is possible with God.

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON DANIEL 2:11 on daniel 2:11
entries.excerpt: The Chaldeans responded, Wherefore it is not possible to know precisely what you [the king] thought. What is humanly possible, they said, you may require from us, but not that which is beyond our nature. You who govern the greatest of all kingdoms must act justly in the governance of that kingdom and require from your subjects that which is possible. That which is now requested is by no mean just. Such knowledge does not belong to human beings.
